terminal/ghostty.analysis: Ghostty scores 95% (140/148) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Core TUI, Rich TUI baselines. Ranks #3 of 13 tested terminals. Missing: CUP with DECOM, Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3), Sixel support in DA1, Sixel graphics, iTerm2 Cell Size Reporting (OSC 1337), iTerm2 Capability Reporting (OSC 1337), Kitty graphics protocol, Feature Reporting (TERM_FEATURES).

terminal/kitty.analysis: Kitty scores 89% (132/148) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Core TUI, Modern TUI, Rich TUI, Unicode baselines. Ranks #8 of 13 tested terminals. Missing 16 features.

terminal/iterm2.analysis: iTerm2 scores 97% (144/148) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Core TUI, Rich TUI baselines. Ranks #2 of 13 tested terminals. Missing: CUP with DECOM, Sixel graphics, iTerm2 Capability Reporting (OSC 1337), Feature Reporting (TERM_FEATURES).

terminal/terminal-app.analysis: Terminal.app scores 89% (132/148) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Core TUI, Modern TUI, Rich TUI, Unicode baselines. Ranks #9 of 13 tested terminals. Missing 16 features.

terminal/warp.analysis: Warp scores 91% (135/148) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Modern TUI, Rich TUI, Unicode baselines. Ranks #5 of 13 tested terminals. Missing 13 features.

terminal/cursor.analysis: Cursor scores 91% (135/148) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Rich TUI, Unicode baselines. Ranks #6 of 13 tested terminals. Missing 13 features.

terminal/vs-code.analysis: VS Code scores 93% (137/148) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Rich TUI, Unicode baselines. Ranks #4 of 13 tested terminals. Missing 11 features.

terminal/vterm.analysis: vterm.js scores 99% (146/147) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Rich TUI baseline. Ranks #1 of 13 tested terminals. Uniquely supports: iTerm2 Capability Reporting (OSC 1337). Missing: Left/Right Margins (DECLRMM, Mode 69).

terminal/vt100-headless.analysis: vt100.js scores 58% (85/147) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Core TUI, Modern TUI, Rich TUI, Unicode baselines. Ranks #13 of 13 tested terminals. Missing 62 features.

terminal/alacritty.analysis: Alacritty scores 88% (129/147) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Core TUI, Modern TUI, Rich TUI, Unicode baselines. Ranks #10 of 13 tested terminals. Uniquely missing (all other terminals pass): Reverse Wrap (Mode 45), Erase screen (ED 2). Missing 18 features.

terminal/wezterm.analysis: WezTerm scores 90% (132/147) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Core TUI, Modern TUI, Rich TUI, Unicode baselines. Ranks #7 of 13 tested terminals. Uniquely missing (all other terminals pass): CUP at screen boundaries. Missing 15 features.

terminal/xterm-js.analysis: xterm.js scores 83% (122/147) on the terminfo.dev feature matrix, with gaps in the Modern TUI, Rich TUI, Unicode baselines. Ranks #12 of 13 tested terminals. Missing 25 features.

terminal/vt52-historical.analysis: DEC VT52 (1975) was manufactured by Digital Equipment Corporation (DEC). First DEC video terminal with cursor addressing. This is a historical reference entry — no automated probe data is available for this terminal.

terminal/vt100-historical.analysis: DEC VT100 (1978) was manufactured by Digital Equipment Corporation (DEC). Defined the escape sequence grammar used by every modern terminal. This is a historical reference entry — no automated probe data is available for this terminal.

terminal/vt220-historical.analysis: DEC VT220 (1983) was manufactured by Digital Equipment Corporation (DEC). Introduced editing operations (ICH/DCH/IL/DL) used by every TUI application. This is a historical reference entry — no automated probe data is available for this terminal.

terminal/xterm-historical.analysis: xterm (1984) was manufactured by MIT / Thomas Dickey. De facto reference implementation — defined most 'standard' terminal behavior. This is a historical reference entry — no automated probe data is available for this terminal.

terminal/vt510-historical.analysis: DEC VT510 (1993) was manufactured by Digital Equipment Corporation (DEC). Reference manual remains the most cited terminal specification. This is a historical reference entry — no automated probe data is available for this terminal.

baseline/core.analysis: 6 of 13 tested terminals achieve 100% Core TUI compliance. Lagging: vt100.js (89%), WezTerm (97%), Ghostty (99%). Most commonly missing: CUP with DECOM, EL erases with bg color, Faint/dim (SGR 2).

baseline/modern.analysis: 5 of 13 tested terminals achieve 100% Modern TUI compliance. Lagging: vt100.js (38%), Cursor (87%), xterm.js (87%). Most commonly missing: Clipboard access (OSC 52), Foreground color query (OSC 10), Background color query (OSC 11).

baseline/rich.analysis: 0 of 13 tested terminals achieve 100% Rich TUI compliance. Lagging: vt100.js (5%), Cursor (24%), xterm.js (24%). Most commonly missing: iTerm2 Capability Reporting (OSC 1337), Kitty graphics protocol, iTerm2 Cell Size Reporting (OSC 1337).

baseline/unicode.analysis: 3 of 13 tested terminals achieve 100% Unicode compliance. Lagging: vt100.js (38%), Kitty (63%), Terminal.app (63%). Most commonly missing: Variation selector 16 (emoji presentation), Emoji ZWJ sequences (2 cols), Grapheme Cluster Cursor Movement.

compare/ghostty-vs-kitty.analysis: Ghostty scores 95% (140/148) vs Kitty at 89% (132/148). Ghostty leads by 6 percentage points. Only in Ghostty: Mode Report (DECRPM), Request Setting (DECRQSS), Query Terminfo (XTGETTCAP), Terminal Version (XTVERSION), Emoji ZWJ sequences (2 cols) and 4 more. Only in Kitty: Sixel graphics.

compare/ghostty-vs-iterm2.analysis: Ghostty scores 95% (140/148) vs iTerm2 at 97% (144/148). iTerm2 leads by 2 percentage points. Only in iTerm2: Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3), Sixel support in DA1, iTerm2 Cell Size Reporting (OSC 1337), Kitty graphics protocol.

compare/ghostty-vs-warp.analysis: Ghostty scores 95% (140/148) vs Warp at 91% (135/148). Ghostty leads by 4 percentage points. Only in Ghostty: Mode Report (DECRPM), Request Setting (DECRQSS), Query Terminfo (XTGETTCAP), Emoji ZWJ sequences (2 cols), Variation selector 16 (emoji presentation) and 1 more. Only in Warp: CUP with DECOM.

compare/ghostty-vs-terminal-app.analysis: Ghostty scores 95% (140/148) vs Terminal.app at 89% (132/148). Ghostty leads by 6 percentage points. Only in Ghostty: Mode Report (DECRPM), Request Setting (DECRQSS), Query Terminfo (XTGETTCAP), Terminal Version (XTVERSION), Emoji ZWJ sequences (2 cols) and 4 more. Only in Terminal.app: Sixel graphics.

compare/iterm2-vs-kitty.analysis: iTerm2 scores 97% (144/148) vs Kitty at 89% (132/148). iTerm2 leads by 8 percentage points. Only in iTerm2: Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3), Mode Report (DECRPM), Request Setting (DECRQSS), Query Terminfo (XTGETTCAP), Terminal Version (XTVERSION) and 8 more. Only in Kitty: Sixel graphics.

compare/kitty-vs-warp.analysis: Kitty scores 89% (132/148) vs Warp at 91% (135/148). Warp leads by 2 percentage points. Only in Kitty: Sixel graphics. Only in Warp: CUP with DECOM, Terminal Version (XTVERSION), Origin mode (DECOM), Kitty keyboard protocol.

compare/iterm2-vs-terminal-app.analysis: iTerm2 scores 97% (144/148) vs Terminal.app at 89% (132/148). iTerm2 leads by 8 percentage points. Only in iTerm2: Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3), Mode Report (DECRPM), Request Setting (DECRQSS), Query Terminfo (XTGETTCAP), Terminal Version (XTVERSION) and 8 more. Only in Terminal.app: Sixel graphics.

compare/iterm2-vs-warp.analysis: iTerm2 scores 97% (144/148) vs Warp at 91% (135/148). iTerm2 leads by 6 percentage points. Only in iTerm2: Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3), Mode Report (DECRPM), Request Setting (DECRQSS), Query Terminfo (XTGETTCAP), Emoji ZWJ sequences (2 cols) and 5 more. Only in Warp: CUP with DECOM.

compare/cursor-vs-vs-code.analysis: Cursor scores 91% (135/148) vs VS Code at 93% (137/148). VS Code leads by 2 percentage points. Only in VS Code: Terminal Version (XTVERSION), Kitty keyboard protocol.

compare/terminal-app-vs-warp.analysis: Terminal.app scores 89% (132/148) vs Warp at 91% (135/148). Warp leads by 2 percentage points. Only in Terminal.app: Sixel graphics. Only in Warp: CUP with DECOM, Terminal Version (XTVERSION), Origin mode (DECOM), Kitty keyboard protocol.

sgr.analysis: The SGR (Text Styling) category covers 29 features. Top performers (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Warp and 7 more. Common gaps: Faint/dim (SGR 2) (1 terminal fails), Italic (SGR 3) (1 terminal fails), Double underline (SGR 21) (1 terminal fails).

cursor.analysis: The Cursor category covers 17 features. Top performers (100%): Warp, Cursor, VS Code, Cursor, vterm.js and 1 more. Common gaps: CUP with DECOM (5 terminals fail), Cursor shape (DECSCUSR) (1 terminal fails), Reverse Wrap (Mode 45) (1 terminal fails).

text.analysis: The Text category covers 16 features. Top performers (100%): Ghostty, iTerm2, vterm.js. Common gaps: Variation selector 16 (emoji presentation) (10 terminals fail), Emoji ZWJ sequences (2 cols) (5 terminals fail), Regional indicator flags (2 cols) (4 terminals fail).

erase.analysis: The Erase category covers 11 features. Top performers (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Warp and 5 more. Common gaps: Selective Erase (DECSED) (3 terminals fail), EL erases with bg color (2 terminals fail), Erase screen (ED 2) (1 terminal fails).

editing.analysis: The Editing category covers 5 features. Top performers (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Warp and 7 more. Common gaps: Repeat character (REP) (1 terminal fails).

modes.analysis: The Modes category covers 15 features. Top performers (100%): Ghostty, iTerm2, Warp, Cursor, VS Code and 2 more. Common gaps: Left/Right Margins (DECLRMM, Mode 69) (4 terminals fail), Origin mode (DECOM) (2 terminals fail), Enter alt screen (DECSET 1049) (1 terminal fails).

scrollback.analysis: The Scrollback category covers 9 features. Top performers (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Warp and 7 more. Common gaps: Alt screen separate scrollback (1 terminal fails).

reset.analysis: The Reset category covers 4 features. All 13 tested terminals achieve 100%.

extensions.analysis: The Extensions category covers 19 features. Top performers (100%): vterm.js. Common gaps: iTerm2 Capability Reporting (OSC 1337) (12 terminals fail), Kitty graphics protocol (11 terminals fail), iTerm2 Cell Size Reporting (OSC 1337) (11 terminals fail).

charsets.analysis: The Character Sets category covers 4 features. All 13 tested terminals achieve 100%.

device.analysis: The Device Status category covers 9 features. Top performers (100%): vterm.js, Alacritty, WezTerm. Common gaps: Tertiary Device Attributes (DA3) (9 terminals fail), Query Terminfo (XTGETTCAP) (8 terminals fail), Feature Reporting (TERM_FEATURES) (7 terminals fail).

input.analysis: The Input Protocols category covers 6 features. Top performers (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Warp and 3 more. Common gaps: urxvt Mouse Reporting (1015) (5 terminals fail), modifyOtherKeys (3 terminals fail), CSI u Key Encoding (3 terminals fail).

unicode.analysis: Unicode defines 10 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 76%. Full compliance (100%): Ghostty, iTerm2, vterm.js. Lowest: vt100.js at 40% (4/10).

ecma-48.analysis: ECMA-48 Standard defines 60 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 99%. Full compliance (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Warp and 5 more. Lowest: vt100.js at 90% (54/60).

vt100.analysis: VT100 defines 50 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 98%. Full compliance (100%): Warp, Cursor, VS Code, Cursor, vterm.js and 1 more. Lowest: vt100.js at 94% (47/50).

vt220.analysis: VT220 defines 4 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 92%. Full compliance (100%): Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, Terminal.app, Warp and 5 more. Lowest: vt100.js at 50% (2/4).

vt510.analysis: VT510 defines 10 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 83%. Full compliance (100%): iTerm2, Alacritty, WezTerm. Lowest: vt100.js at 50% (5/10).

dec-private-modes.analysis: DEC Private Modes defines 22 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 94%. Full compliance (100%): Warp, Cursor, VS Code, Cursor, vterm.js and 1 more. Lowest: vt100.js at 59% (13/22).

xterm-extensions.analysis: Xterm Extensions defines 33 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 86%. Full compliance (100%): Ghostty, iTerm2, vterm.js. Lowest: vt100.js at 21% (7/33).

kitty-extensions.analysis: Kitty Extensions defines 6 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 73%. Full compliance (100%): iTerm2, vterm.js. Lowest: vt100.js at 0% (0/6).

osc.analysis: Operating System Commands (OSC) defines 13 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 66%. Full compliance (100%): vterm.js. Lowest: vt100.js at 15% (2/13).

sixel.analysis: Sixel Graphics defines 2 features in the terminfo.dev matrix. Average adoption across terminals: 23%. Full compliance (100%): vterm.js. Lowest: xterm.js at 0% (0/2).

framework/silvery.analysis: Silvery requires the Modern TUI baseline — 5 of 13 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: Ghostty, iTerm2, Cursor, VS Code, vterm.js. Same baseline tier (Modern TUI) as Textual, Bubbletea, Ratatui. Other frameworks target: Ink, Blessed (Core TUI).

framework/ink.analysis: Ink requires the Core TUI baseline — 6 of 13 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: Warp, Cursor, VS Code, Cursor, vterm.js, xterm.js. Same baseline tier (Core TUI) as Blessed. Other frameworks target: Silvery, Textual, Bubbletea, Ratatui (Modern TUI).

framework/textual.analysis: Textual requires the Modern TUI baseline — 5 of 13 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: Ghostty, iTerm2, Cursor, VS Code, vterm.js. Same baseline tier (Modern TUI) as Silvery, Bubbletea, Ratatui. Other frameworks target: Ink, Blessed (Core TUI).

framework/bubbletea.analysis: Bubbletea requires the Modern TUI baseline — 5 of 13 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: Ghostty, iTerm2, Cursor, VS Code, vterm.js. Same baseline tier (Modern TUI) as Silvery, Textual, Ratatui. Other frameworks target: Ink, Blessed (Core TUI).

framework/ratatui.analysis: Ratatui requires the Modern TUI baseline — 5 of 13 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: Ghostty, iTerm2, Cursor, VS Code, vterm.js. Same baseline tier (Modern TUI) as Silvery, Textual, Bubbletea. Other frameworks target: Ink, Blessed (Core TUI).

framework/blessed.analysis: Blessed requires the Core TUI baseline — 6 of 13 tested terminals are fully compatible. Compatible: Warp, Cursor, VS Code, Cursor, vterm.js, xterm.js. Same baseline tier (Core TUI) as Ink. Other frameworks target: Silvery, Textual, Bubbletea, Ratatui (Modern TUI).

standards-index.analysis: Terminfo.dev tracks 10 terminal standards, from ECMA-48 (1976) to Kitty Extensions (2017). Highest average adoption: ECMA-48 Standard at 99%. Lowest: Sixel Graphics at 23%. 10 of 13 terminals achieve 100% ECMA-48 Standard compliance. 6 of 13 terminals achieve 100% VT100 compliance.

features-index.analysis: The terminfo.dev matrix covers 148 features across 13 categories. Best-supported category: Reset (100% average). Most challenging: Extensions (61%). 79 features are universally supported by all tested terminals. 1 feature has zero support across all tested terminals.
